thoughtHistory.thought: Starting a deep review of the screening section. I need to map all screening-related files first, then inspect each for bugs, broken integrations, incorrect responses, missing logic, and import issues. Screening involves: quick-screen (name matching), batch-screen, screening/run, ongoing/run, screen/batch, payment-screen, wire/screen, and the brain modules (quickScreen, matching, translit, redlines, cognitive-amplifier). Let me enumerate all relevant files.

50+ screening-related files found. Priority targets for deep review:
1. Core routes: quick-screen/route.ts, screening/run/route.ts, screen/batch/route.ts, batch-screen/route.ts, ongoing/run/route.ts
2. Brain modules: quick-screen.ts, matching.ts, translit.ts, redlines.ts, cognitive-amplifier.ts
3. Specialized: payment-screen, wire/screen, pep-screening-enhance, pf-screener, tf-screener

Strategy: Read the core screening routes first (quick-screen, screening/run, screen/batch), then brain engine, then specialized routes. Check for: import correctness, response shape consistency, error handling, missing fields, auth, logic flaws.

Findings so far in quick-screen/route.ts and screening/run/route.ts:

BUG 1 — screening/run/route.ts has DUPLICATE auto-case creation block (lines 328-360 and 362-420). The same fire-and-forget fetch to /api/hs-cases runs TWICE per screening hit. The second block (lines 362-420) is the more complete version (it handles enrich + rescreen-queue). The first block (lines 328-360) is a dupe and must be removed.

BUG 2 — screening/run/route.ts: import at line 28 still uses relative src/ path:
  import { quickScreen as brainQuickScreen } from '../../../../../src/brain/quick-screen.js';
This is fine (we fixed dist/src → src), but it should ideally use the @brain alias like quick-screen/route.ts does. NOT a bug per se — just inconsistency. Low priority.

BUG 3 — quick-screen/route.ts line 955: listIds referenced as finalResult.listIds but QuickScreenResult does not appear to have a listIds field. Let me verify this.

Next: check the QuickScreenResult type and screen/batch/route.ts.

screen/batch/route.ts BUG: Line 268 accesses raw.results instead of raw.hits. QuickScreenResult has hits[], not results[]. The correct field is hits, not results. Also hits[0].name is wrong — it should be hits[0].candidateName. This means the screen/batch endpoint always returns hitCount=0 and lists=[] because it reads the wrong field from the result object.

Specifically at line 268:
  const hits = ((raw as { results?: HitLike[] }).results ?? []).filter(...)
Should be:
  const hits = ((raw as { hits?: HitLike[] }).hits ?? []).filter(...)

And at line 283, topHit?.name should be topHit?.candidateName since QuickScreenHit has candidateName not name.

Also at line 267, raw.topScore is accessed correctly.

Let me verify the QuickScreenHit type.

More findings:

BUG 4 — screening/run/route.ts DUPLICATE auto-case block confirmed. Lines 328-360 and 362-420 are completely duplicated. The first block (328-360) creates a case but never triggers the enrich step or rescreen-queue. The second block (362-420) is more complete. The first block must be removed.

wire/screen/route.ts looks correct — uses quickScreen with .hits properly.
screening-history/route.ts looks correct.
screening/health/route.ts looks correct — uses static import for quickScreen (good, no cold-start dynamic import needed for health probes).
payment-screen/route.ts looks correct so far.

Next: check the pep-screening-enhance, pf-screener, tf-screener, and pf-screener for issues. Also check batch-screen/route.ts more thoroughly (it has the score mapping at lines 33-39 using threshold 50 which doesn't match quick-screen/route.ts's tier thresholds). Let me check if scoreToBand is consistent with QuickScreenResult.severity.

BUG 6 — screening/enhanced/route.ts uses hit.name throughout (lines 160, 193, 201, 203, 219, 221, 224) but QuickScreenHit has 'candidateName' not 'name'. However, the ScreeningHit interface defined in this file (line 37) uses 'name?: string' and the route calls /api/screening/run first then processes the result — the response from screening/run includes the raw QuickScreenHit objects where the field IS candidateName. So if screening/run returns hits with candidateName, but enhanced tries to access hit.name, it will always be undefined. The dedup at line 219 will key everything under '' (empty string after lowercasing undefined).

BUT wait — the route calls callScreeningRun which calls /api/screening/run, and that route returns the result from brainQuickScreen which uses candidateName. The enhanced route then accesses hit.name — this is broken.

Let me now check all the bugs I've found and compile the fix list. I'll also quickly check the ongoing/run route for screening-history writing correctness.

ongoing/run/route.ts looks correct — uses candidateName properly throughout, correctly writes to screening-history/<id>/<runAt> with correct shape {at, topScore, severity, lists, hits}. No bugs here.

Let me now compile my full bug list and implement fixes:

CONFIRMED BUGS:
1. screening/run/route.ts — DUPLICATE auto-case block (lines 328-360 = dupe of 362-420). Remove first block.
2. screen/batch/route.ts — reads raw.results instead of raw.hits (line 268). Also topHit?.name should be topHit?.candidateName (line 283).
3. screen/batch/route.ts — scoreToBand thresholds wrong: should be >=95 critical, >=85 high, >=70 medium, else low.
4. screening/enhanced/route.ts — uses hit.name instead of hit.candidateName throughout. Need to update ScreeningHit interface to use candidateName and update all references.

Let me now implement these fixes.
